input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known breaking change — `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` in API 30+. In .NET 10 (which targets newer Android APIs), MAUI moved to using `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_PAN` or edge-to-edge insets.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI's evolution.

In .NET 10 / Android 15+, `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` is fully gone. 

The solution is to use `WindowInsets` to monitor keyboard visibility and dynamically adjust your layout's padding in response, since the old resize behavior is no longer available. I'll walk through a practical implementation.
